latest.title: Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
latest.content:
14 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2466A
Avon Technologies Plc
14 April 2026
14 April 2026
AVON TECHNOLOGIES PLC
NOTICE OF RESULTS AND INVESTOR PRESENTATION VIA INVESTOR MEET COMPANY
Avon Technologies plc will announce its Interim Results for the six months ended 31 March 2026 on 13 May 2026.
Analyst & Investor Presentation
Jos Sclater, Chief Executive Officer and Rich Cashin, Chief Financial Officer, will host a presentation for investors and sell-side analysts at 09:00 BST on the day.
Please contact avontechnologies@sodali.com to attend the presentation in person or receive the dial-in details for the webcast.
Retail Investor Presentation
Jos Sclater and Rich Cashin will also provide a live presentation and Q&A for retail investors via Investor Meet Company on 15 May 2026 at 12:00 BST.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet AVON TECHNOLOGIES PLC via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/avon-technologies-plc/register-investor
Questions can be submitted pre-event via the Investor Meet Company dashboard, or at any time during the live presentation.
For further enquiries, please contact:
Avon Technologies plc
Gabriella Colley, Corporate Affairs Director
investor.relations@avon-technologiesplc.com
+44 7891 206 239
Sodali & Co
Pete LambieTilly Abraham
avontechnologies@sodali.com
+44 7855 432 699
About Avon Technologies plc:
Avon Technologies plc makes products that are trusted to protect the world's militaries and first responders.
Our mission is to provide unparalleled protection for those who protect us, giving them the confidence to tackle challenging situations and helping them get home safe.
The Group trades through two Strategic Business Units; Avon Protection; our respiratory and integrated protection systems business and Team Wendy, our helmet systems business. We are renowned for our innovative thinking and our steadfast approach to manufacturing unrivalled products.
For further information, please visit our website
www.avon-technologiesplc.com
Legal Entity Identifier: 213800JM1AN62REBWA71
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
8 Apr 2026 11:47
RNS Number : 6699Z
Avon Technologies Plc
08 April 2026
8 April 2026
Avon Technologies plc
("Avon" or "the Group")
Directors'/PDMR Shareholdings
Share Incentive Plan
Avon Technologies plc was notified on 8 April 2026 that the following purchases of Avon Technologies plc ordinary shares were made by Equiniti Share Plan Trustees Limited on 7 April 2026 on behalf of two of the Group's persons discharging managerial responsibilities ('PDMR') pursuant to the Group's Share Incentive Plan (SIP).
The SIP is an all-employee share plan arrangement approved by HM Revenue and Customs, under which employees are able to buy ordinary shares in Avon Technologies plc using deductions from salary in each monthly pay period.
This notification is made in order to satisfy both section 793 of the Companies Act 2006 and the requirements of the EU Market Abuse Regulation.
The information contained in the below notifications are disclosed in accordance with Article 19 of the EU Market Abuse Regulation.

company_30s.what_they_do: AVON.L is a UK‑based defence technology specialist.  Its core products are protective systems for military and civil use, including ballistic‑resistant gear, electronic counter‑measure devices and integrated protection solutions for vehicles and personnel.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Defence spending is a large, relatively inelastic market.  Governments worldwide are updating fleets and security protocols, creating a steady demand for advanced protection tech.  Avon’s niche focus on modular, upgrade‑friendly systems gives it a competitive edge in both commercial and government contracts.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently secured a sizeable US Department of Defence order and is in the process of delivering the Avon Protection unit.  It has been trading at very low levels after a sharp sell‑off, but the fundamentals – a growing defence contract pipeline and a solid balance sheet – suggest a potential turnaround.  The share price sits well below the pre‑crash valuation, indicating a deep discount.

rns_narrative: AVON has been busy with governance and financial updates: share‑holding disclosures, AGM results, and dividend announcements.  These are routine but signal that the board is staying compliant and that investors are still receiving returns.

The most compelling story is the new DoD contract and the company’s active role in defence procurement.  This demonstrates that Avon’s products are being accepted by key buyers and that the firm is moving from a niche supplier to a recognized partner.
There is a subtle shift toward stabilization: the company is moving from a crisis‑mode of survival to a growth‑mode of contract execution.  The narrative is now about execution rather than survival.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the chatter is thin but focused on defence wins.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices highlight the new US contract and the company’s potential to benefit from rising defence budgets.  They see Avon as a small‑cap catalyst waiting for the next order book.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the company’s thin earnings history and the fact that it’s still a relatively new player in a highly regulated market.  They worry about the risk of contract delays or cost overruns.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has shifted from defensive to mildly optimistic as contract news emerges, but overall engagement remains low.  The community is still on the sidelines, waiting for a clear price move.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – the public has not yet noticed Avon’s story, giving the stock a surprise edge when the next catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming quarterly earnings report – a positive surprise in revenue or margin could trigger a bounce.
Any announcement of additional defence orders or extensions to the current DoD contract.
A volume spike at the 10‑day low or a break of the 20‑day moving average, signalling renewed buying interest.
A change in director shareholdings that could indicate confidence from insiders.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained uptick in trading volume and a clear breakout above the current support level.  A 5‑day moving average crossing above the 20‑day average would be a strong confirmation.  Also keep an eye on any regulatory filings that could affect the company’s ability to deliver contracts.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of its recovery – the crash has been deep and the price is at a low that is likely below the true support.  The APEX score suggests a tight compression, so a breakout could be imminent if the above catalysts materialise.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (likely >70%) coupled with a fresh defence contract provides a classic mean‑reversion setup.  If the stock recovers to 50% of its pre‑crash level, the upside is roughly double the current price, delivering a 100% return on the trade.  Historical data for RED signals shows an average return of 160% for similar deep crashes, so the upside potential is significant.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still has thin earnings and a limited order pipeline.  A missed contract deadline or a regulatory hold could stall the recovery.  The worst‑case is a return to zero if the company cannot deliver on its obligations, wiping out the position.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the initial investment (the stock could fall to zero).  Potential gain is at least 2× the entry price, giving a risk‑reward ratio of 1:2 or better.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a confirmed breakout above the 20‑day moving average with volume at least 2× the 10‑day average.  Size the position to 5% of the portfolio and set a stop 5% below the entry to cap downside.  Trail the stop by 3% once the price has moved 10% in your favour.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the next earnings release or a clear volume spike at the 10‑day low.  Watch for a 3‑day moving average crossing above the 20‑day average – that’s the trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or a major contract is cancelled, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AVON.L is a deep‑crashed defence tech stock with a clear new contract catalyst; a contrarian bullish stance offers high upside with a limited downside if the price recovers.
